summary: Added a Swift client-side encoded video receive queue with payload byte-count validation, SHA-256 hash validation, depth-one stale-frame dropping, deterministic timeline events, and XCTest coverage. The node remains synthetic/in-memory and makes no live network, capture, decode, render, or latency claim.

acceptanceEvidence.criterion: Swift client core validates incoming encoded frame metadata against payload byte count and SHA-256 payload hash.
Queue depth is bounded to one ready frame and accepting a newer frame drops stale queued frame evidence deterministically.
Receive and dequeue timeline events can be emitted as Codable evidence for later latency reports.
XCTest coverage exercises valid enqueue/dequeue, byte-count mismatch, hash mismatch, stale-frame drop, and stable timeline JSON.
Evidence is saved under evidence/m3-mac-video-receive-queue and reports under reports/qd/m3-mac-video-receive-queue.
No live network, Linux capture, VideoToolbox decode, Metal render, or cross-device latency claim is made.

realWorldValidation.evidence: Synthetic in-memory Mac receive queue only; no live network or cross-device claim.
